title: CKD review with NSAID risk and medication interaction concern

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Your kidney function is lower this time. Have you started any new medicines?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: I have been taking ibuprofen most days for my knee. I am also on ramipril, metformin, and furosemide.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Any dehydration, vomiting, reduced urine, swelling, or shortness of breath?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: I had a stomach bug last week and drank less, but urine is back to normal. No shortness of breath.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: The ibuprofen plus ramipril and furosemide can worsen kidney function, especially after dehydration. Stop ibuprofen. Use paracetamol and topical anti-inflammatory instead. We will repeat UEC/eGFR in one week, check blood pressure today, and review metformin if kidney function falls further.

candidate_notes.note: CKD review with reduced eGFR. Recent daily ibuprofen for knee pain while taking ramipril, furosemide, and metformin; recent gastroenteritis/dehydration. No current reduced urine, swelling, or SOB. Impression NSAID/ACEi/diuretic renal risk after dehydration. Stop ibuprofen. Use paracetamol and topical NSAID. Check BP today. Repeat UEC/eGFR in 1 week; review metformin if renal function worsens.
Kidney function slightly reduced. Patient using ibuprofen for knee pain. Advised hydration and repeat blood test. Continue current regular medications.

S: Lower kidney function, recent stomach bug, daily ibuprofen for knee. Medications include ramipril, metformin, furosemide. No current oliguria, swelling, SOB. A: CKD deterioration possibly dehydration/NSAID related. P: Stop ibuprofen, paracetamol/topical therapy, repeat UEC/eGFR in one week.

Reduced renal function in context of daily ibuprofen plus ramipril and furosemide after dehydration illness. Metformin also noted. No current reduced urine, oedema, or SOB. Stop ibuprofen. Use paracetamol/topical anti-inflammatory. BP today. Repeat UEC/eGFR in 1 week and review metformin if eGFR declines further.

CDSS medication-safety alert: NSAID + ACE inhibitor + diuretic renal risk, amplified by recent dehydration. Patient taking ibuprofen most days with ramipril/furosemide/metformin. Stop ibuprofen; paracetamol and topical option instead. Check BP, repeat UEC/eGFR in one week, and reassess metformin if kidney function worsens. Safety advice for reduced urine, swelling, SOB, dehydration.
